intro: AppsFlyer and Branch both sit in the Mobile Attribution / MMP space. Both tools position themselves for mobile, so the decision rarely comes down to surface features. In practice, AppsFlyer — Mobile attribution and marketing analytics platform with deterministic matching, deep links, and Privacy Sandbox support. Branch — Mobile linking and attribution platform with deep linking, deferred deep linking, web-to-app journeys, and MMP capabilities. AppsFlyer runs on usage from $0.05-0.07/install (Growth); Branch runs on subscription. Both tools list mobile apps with paid acquisition as a headline strength, which is why pricing and lock-in usually decide the call. Both tools surface overlapping when-to-use scenarios, including mobile apps with paid acquisition. The honest trade-off: AppsFlyer is not built for web-only products, and Branch is not the right call for web-only products, so neither is a universal default. Transparency scores land at 3/5 for AppsFlyer and 3/5 for Branch, which reflects how openly each tool documents pricing, limits, and terms.
quick_take: AppsFlyer is for mobile; Branch overlaps on the same job; decide based on which use case matches.
verdict_a: Choose AppsFlyer if mobile and the $0.05-0.07/install (Growth) entry price fits your budget.
verdict_b: Choose Branch if mobile and moderate platform coupling is acceptable.

faq.a: The listed pricing for AppsFlyer is usage from $0.05-0.07/install (Growth) and for Branch is subscription, which doesn't give a clean apples-to-apples cost. Unit economics and plan structure differ enough that only your real volume answers this. Price both against the same workload.

faq.q: Can I migrate from AppsFlyer to Branch?
faq.a: Technically you can move between any two tools in the same category, but effort varies: leaving AppsFlyer is moderate effort at medium lock-in (Medium — attribution data tied to to AppsFlyer ecosystem), and onboarding to Branch involves medium lock-in (Deep linking, SDK tied but standard). Plan for data export, re-integration, and a parallel-run window rather than a hard cutover.

faq.q: Which has better developer experience, AppsFlyer or Branch?
faq.a: Both AppsFlyer and Branch score 5/5 on developer experience in our data, so there's no clear winner on that axis alone. The real differentiator usually lands in SDK fit for your language, error handling, and docs depth. A half-day integration spike tells you more than the score.

faq.q: Is Branch a good alternative to AppsFlyer?
faq.a: Yes, Branch targets the same job AppsFlyer does — mobile — so functionally it slots in as an alternative. Pricing model (subscription vs usage from $0.05-0.07/install (Growth)) and lock-in (medium lock-in (Deep linking, SDK tied but standard) vs medium lock-in (Medium — attribution data tied to to AppsFlyer ecosystem)) usually decide which is the better fit. Validate on your actual workload. Note: Branch is explicitly not for web-only products.
